_comment: Drift burn-down categorization. Regenerate with: node scripts/categorize-drift.mjs

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Add nullable idempotencyKey TEXT column to equipmentTelemetrySqlite (additive, no backfill needed — used by the cloud-side dedupe only).
medium.risk: Low. Column is write-side only; reads ignore it.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Add orgId TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default-org-id' to equipmentLifecycleSqlite + create index. Backfill via single UPDATE before flipping to NOT NULL.
medium.risk: Medium. Existing offline DBs need a one-shot backfill migration; flipping NOT NULL without backfill breaks INSERTs.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Same as equipmentLifecycle (orgId column + index + backfill).
medium.risk: Medium. Identical migration shape.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Same as equipmentLifecycle (orgId column + index + backfill).
medium.risk: Medium.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Same as equipmentLifecycle (orgId column + index + backfill).
medium.risk: Medium.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Drop reservedAt from SQLite (or add to PG); reconcile quantity type (PG uses numeric, SQLite uses real).
medium.risk: Medium. Type drift on quantity is the real bug — choose numeric and migrate with cast.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Add schematicLayout JSON column to vesselsSqlite. The id/orgId false positives in sqliteOnly come from the validator picking up shadowed shorthand declarations — verified by running scripts/validate-dual-schema.mjs against the canonical PG copy.
medium.risk: Low for schematicLayout (purely UI metadata). The id/orgId 'sqliteOnly' is a known false positive in the diff tool; safe to leave allowlisted.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Add orgId to PG side; rename sqliteOnly recordId → maintenanceRecordId and scheduleId → maintenanceScheduleId to match PG conventions.
medium.risk: Medium. Renames need a coordinated repo + handler change.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Add rejectedQuantity (integer) and rejectionReason (text) to SQLite; reconcile receivedQuantity type drift (PG numeric vs SQLite real).
medium.risk: Low. Both columns are nullable; type drift on receivedQuantity has no known runtime consumers comparing across drivers.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Rename SQLite 'isActive' → 'active' to match PG; add maxLevel INTEGER DEFAULT 5 column to SQLite.
medium.risk: Low. Rename touches one repo (db/crew/db-skills.ts); maxLevel is additive with default.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Add scope TEXT to SQLite; promote sqliteOnly actualCost/notes to PG side (or drop from SQLite if unused).
medium.risk: Medium. drydockWindow is currently cloud-only in production; offline mirror is rarely exercised.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Add description TEXT to PG; reconcile id/version type drift (PG: integer/text vs SQLite: text/integer). This table is internal bookkeeping — a one-shot rebuild is acceptable.
medium.risk: Low. Internal table, no FK dependencies.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Add checklistItems (jsonb), estimatedDurationMinutes (integer), intervalDays (integer), intervalHours (integer) to SQLite. Maintenance template sync is one-direction (cloud → vessel) so SQLite must be the superset.
medium.risk: Low. Additive nullables.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Standardize on the SQLite naming convention: rename PG.requestId → idempotencyKey + requestHash + orgId. PG version is newer but uses ad-hoc names that don't compose with the sync layer.
medium.risk: Medium. Touches the idempotency middleware — needs a coordinated rollout.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Cloud-only at present; either add the table to PG schema (preferred) or remove from sqlite-schema if abandoned. sqliteOnly fields (id/orgId/reason/sheetType) are the full table.
medium.risk: High. Used by the offline crew-rest editor. Removing it breaks Tauri/Capacitor offline mode.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: sqliteOnly fields (lastModifiedBy/lastModifiedDevice/version) are sync metadata; either port to PG (preferred for full audit) or move into a dedicated work_orders_sync table.
medium.risk: Medium. Affects the sync journal compaction logic in server/sync.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Add maxValue/minValue (numeric) and units (text) to SQLite; rename sqliteOnly 'code' → 'type' (or whichever PG uses). The orgId mismatch is sensor-types being global vs per-tenant — needs a product decision.
medium.risk: Medium. Product decision required for orgId.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Field renames: alertNotificationId → alertId, createdBy → commentedBy, createdByName drop. SQLite was built off an earlier API contract.
medium.risk: Medium. Renames touch the alert-comments repo + UI consumers.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Same as sheetLock — cloud-only, must be ported to PG (preferred) to enable cloud-side audit of crew-rest edits.
medium.risk: High. Same offline dependency.

medium.action: Align column names/types — small schema patch
medium.resolution: Field renames: assignmentDate → date, schedulerRunId → runId. SQLite was built off an earlier draft.
medium.risk: Low. scheduler-unfilled is cloud-only in production; SQLite rows are never written.

_annotation_note: Each 'medium' drift entry now carries 'resolution' (safe path forward) and 'risk' (what breaks if applied blindly). 5 SQLite-orgId entries share a single migration shape; 5 are renames; 4 are additive cloud-side ports; 2 are cloud-only tables that must be promoted to PG; 4 require small product decisions. None are auto-fixable via `db:push --force` — each needs a hand-written migration.
